title: STUDIES ON THE METAL COMPLEXES OF HYDROXAMIC ACIDS. PART V.  CO-DETERMINATION OF IRON AND MANGANESE WITH  ANTHRANILOHYDROXAMIC ACID
author: R. L. DUTTA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Anthranilohydroxamic acid (2-aminobenzohydroxamic acid) has been studied as a calorimetric reagent for co-determination of iron and manganese. Iron (ferric or ferrous) has been estimated by measuring the optical density of the orange-red complex ( \(\lambda\)  max  450 mμ) extracted from aqueous medium at pH 4.5 with butyl alcohol. Manganese is then determined in the aqueous laver with the same reagent at pH above 9.2, when a wine-red complex ( \(\lambda\)  max  490-500 mμ) is farmed. A smooth separation and co-determination of iron and manganese have thus been possible. An amount of iron, as high as 250 times that of the manganese, does not offer any difficulty.
steps_raw: Anthranilohydroxamic acid (2-aminobenzohydroxamic acid) has been studied as a calorimetric reagent for co-determination of iron and manganese. Iron (ferric or ferrous) has been estimated by measuring the optical density of the orange-red complex ( \(\lambda\)  max  450 mμ) extracted from aqueous medium at pH 4.5 with butyl alcohol. Manganese is then determined in the aqueous laver with the same reagent at pH above 9.2, when a wine-red complex ( \(\lambda\)  max  490-500 mμ) is farmed. A smooth separation and co-determination of iron and manganese have thus been possible. An amount of iron, as high as 250 times that of the manganese, does not offer any difficulty.
